text: Could you please {{c1::power cycle}} the router by unplugging it for 30 seconds?
explanation: power cycle is a technical verb meaning to turn an electronic device completely off (usually by cutting power) and back on again to reset it.
usage:
Pattern: power cycle [device] (e.g. power cycle the server, power cycle the modem)
  • If your connection drops, try power cycling the access point.
  • I power cycled the switch, which solved the IP conflict.
spanish: ¿Podrías por favor apagar y encender el router desenchufándolo durante 30 segundos?

text: We are currently experiencing a {{c1::service disruption}} affecting our primary database, and our team is working to mitigate it.
explanation: service disruption is the formal corporate term for an outage or downtime. mitigate means to reduce the severity or impact of an ongoing incident.
usage:
Pattern: experience a service disruption | mitigate [incident/impact/risk]
  • The network migration caused a minor service disruption for 10 minutes.
  • We are deploying a hotfix to mitigate the database CPU spikes.
spanish: Actualmente estamos experimentando una interrupción del servicio que afecta a nuestra base de datos principal, y nuestro equipo está trabajando para mitigarlo.

text: The engineer reported: "We are experiencing a major {{c1::infrastructure outage}} due to a {{c2::database bottleneck}}, and we are preparing a root cause analysis."
explanation: Outage means service downtime. **Bottleneck** refers to a point of congestion in a system that slows down performance. **Root cause analysis (RCA)** is the diagnostic process to find the source of the issue.
usage:
Pattern: experience an outage due to [cause] / conduct a root cause analysis
  • The network outage was caused by a configuration error.
  • We conducted a root cause analysis to prevent future failures.
spanish: El ingeniero informó: "Estamos experimentando una interrupción importante de la infraestructura debido a un cuello de botella en la base de datos, y estamos preparando un análisis de causa raíz".

text: Have you tried turning the device off and on again to {{c1::power cycle}} it?
explanation: To 'power cycle' a device means to shut off the power to a hardware piece and then turn it back on, which clears memory and resets state.
usage:
Pattern: power cycle / reboot the router
  • IT support always asks you to power cycle the modem first.
  • Power cycling the switch resolved the packet loss.
spanish: ¿Ha intentado apagar y volver a encender el dispositivo para reiniciar el ciclo de energía?

text: I will open a support ticket for this issue, and our team will {{c1::look into it}} shortly.
explanation: To 'look into' is a common phrasal verb meaning to investigate, examine, or check the status of a problem or request.
usage:
Pattern: look into [issue / ticket / bug]
  • We are looking into the server latency report.
  • I'll look into why your payment was declined.
spanish: Abriré un ticket de soporte para este problema, y nuestro equipo lo revisará/investigará en breve.

text: Please make sure your security credentials are up to date to prevent login {{c1::lockouts}}.
explanation: A 'lockout' is a security state where a user is temporarily or permanently barred from accessing an account after too many failed password attempts.
usage:
Pattern: account lockout / lockout policy
  • Three failed attempts will trigger an automatic account lockout.
  • Contact IT support to resolve your password lockout.
spanish: Por favor, asegúrese de que sus credenciales de seguridad estén actualizadas para evitar bloqueos de inicio de sesión.

text: We are currently experiencing a temporary server outage; we apologize for the {{c1::downtime}}.
explanation: 'Downtime' refers to the period during which a system, computer network, or server is unavailable or offline.
usage:
Pattern: server downtime / system downtime
  • Scheduled maintenance downtime will occur this Sunday.
  • Our service level agreement guarantees less than 0.01% downtime.
spanish: Actualmente estamos experimentando una interrupción temporal del servidor; nos disculpamos por el tiempo de inactividad.

text: Could you please send me a screenshot of the error message to help us {{c1::troubleshoot}} the problem?
explanation: To 'troubleshoot' means to identify, analyze, and resolve technical faults or bugs in a system by following a logical process of elimination.
usage:
Pattern: troubleshoot the problem / technical troubleshooting
  • We need to troubleshoot why the API is returning 500 errors.
  • He is very good at troubleshooting network connection problems.
spanish: ¿Podría por favor enviarme una captura de pantalla del mensaje de error para ayudarnos a diagnosticar/resolver el problema?

text: You need to update your operating system to the latest version to patch these {{c1::security vulnerabilities}}.
explanation: A 'security vulnerability' is a flaw or weakness in software or hardware that malicious actors can exploit to gain unauthorized access or execute code.
usage:
Pattern: patch security vulnerabilities / zero-day vulnerability
  • The patch fixes critical security vulnerabilities in the kernel.
  • Cybercriminals scan the web looking for unpatched security vulnerabilities.
spanish: Necesita actualizar su sistema operativo a la última versión para parchear estas vulnerabilidades de seguridad.

text: Please check your spam folder if you haven't received the password reset link {{c1::in your inbox}}.
explanation: An 'inbox' is the folder where newly received email messages are stored.
usage:
Pattern: in your inbox / email inbox
  • The confirmation code should arrive in your inbox in a minute.
  • My inbox is flooded with spam emails.
spanish: Por favor, revise su carpeta de correo no deseado (spam) si no ha recibido el enlace de restablecimiento de contraseña en su bandeja de entrada.
